access.anon: Can query the public academic catalog and bus timetable through /api/graphql.
access.user: Can use public queries, authenticated Viewer fields, and personal mutations through a trusted same-origin Session.
access.admin: Uses the same Viewer and ordinary personal mutation surfaces as a regular user; GraphQL exposes no admin governance fields or mutations.
access.agent: Direct GraphQL HTTP callers use a bearer token issued for the GraphQL resource and matching feature scopes. MCP agents authenticate with an MCP-resource bearer, then use the versioned registered-operation manifest and run_graphql_operation with the selected operation's exact scopes; arbitrary GraphQL documents are never accepted by MCP.

rules.scoped-mutations: Every personal mutation requires authentication, the matching feature:write OAuth scope for bearer callers, and the shared per-user mutation rate-limit action key used by REST and MCP. Trusted session callers receive the equivalent first-party access.
rules.batch-mutations: Todo and homework batches preserve REST per-item partial-success results. Section subscription batches reuse the shared transaction-aware service. Every GraphQL batch uses the stricter feature:batch-write rate-limit budget, rejects duplicate targets before writing, and relies on GraphQL input coercion to reject unknown fields and non-null ambiguity.
rules.single-endpoint: GraphQL HTTP requests use /api/graphql with GET, POST, and OPTIONS handlers.
rules.production-discovery: GraphiQL and schema introspection are disabled in production.
rules.request-budgets: Requests are limited to 64 KiB, one operation per HTTP request, five seconds, depth 8, 10 top-level fields, 15 aliases, 10 directives, 1000 tokens, and cost 5000; operation cost weights every paginated selection by its effective pageSize, including values supplied through variables.
rules.registered-operation-runner: MCP can execute only checked-in, named, pre-parsed operations from life-ustc://graphql/operations. Unknown operation IDs, undeclared variables, invalid GraphQL variable values, missing exact feature scopes, and unconfirmed mutations are rejected before resolver execution; query/document/source parameters do not exist.
rules.mutation-timeout-ambiguity: A GraphQL timeout or caller cancellation stops waiting for the result but cannot guarantee rollback after a mutation resolver has started. The final state is indeterminate; clients must inspect current state and must not blindly retry a mutation.
rules.registered-operation-coverage: The manifest covers every current public Query root field, each Viewer field as a separate exact-scope operation, and every current ordinary business Mutation root field. Authentication and OAuth protocol operations, admin-only governance, upload raw-byte transfer, subscriptions, and arbitrary documents remain intentionally outside this runner.
rules.pagination: Every GraphQL collection is bounded: Viewer todos, subscribed sections, homeworks, schedules, and exams use PageInput, while nested Schedule.teachers and Exam.examRooms return their own PageInput-backed pages; inputs default to page 1 and pageSize 20, and pageSize must be between 1 and 100.
rules.resolver-boundary: Resolvers call feature-owned services only; they do not issue internal HTTP requests or access Prisma directly.
rules.request-cache: The principal and entity DataLoaders are request-scoped; authentication is resolved once per request and every response is marked no-store.
rules.safe-errors: Expected authentication, authorization, bad-input, not-found, rate-limit, and availability GraphQLErrors retain stable codes and status metadata across HTTP and the MCP registered runner; only trusted GraphQLErrors pass the allowlist, while unexpected or forged resolver failures are masked without internal details.
rules.semantic-observability: Each attempted HTTP or MCP registered GraphQL operation records one sanitized semantic observation with its operation name and type, top-level field count, estimated cost, duration, GraphQL error count, auth mode, and request ID; raw documents, variables, credentials, argument values, and error details are excluded.
rules.viewer-scopes: Query.viewer returns null for an anonymous caller. Its profile, overview, todos, subscribedSections, homeworks, schedules, and exams fields independently require me:read, dashboard:read, todo:read, subscription:read, homework:read, schedule:read, and exam:read for OAuth principals; trusted-origin Sessions use their existing user authority.
rules.viewer-ownership: Viewer collections derive ownership from the authenticated user relation in feature services and never accept sectionIds or internal visibility/shape flags from GraphQL inputs.
rules.zoned-datetime: DateTime inputs accept only valid ISO 8601 datetimes with an explicit Z or numeric timezone offset, with identical coercion for variables and inline literals; schedule and exam DateTime filters normalize to their Asia/Shanghai calendar day before querying @db.Date columns.
rules.schema-evolution: The canonical SDL snapshot is checked for drift and breaking changes in CI.

public-query-endpoint.title: Public Queries and Scoped Personal Mutations

fields: Public semester, course, section, teacher, and bus queries
Authenticated Viewer reads with exact feature:read scopes
Authenticated todo and homework CRUD, todo/homework completion batches, section subscription batches, dashboard pin, bus preference, description, and comment mutations
GraphQL-audience bearer tokens require matching feature scopes
MCP executes only approved IDs from the registered-operation manifest

queries.name: viewer
notes: Returns null for anonymous requests; each selected Viewer child field enforces its exact feature read scope.

resources.name: graphql_operations_manifest
resources.description: Versioned registered GraphQL operation metadata, including variables, exact scopes, and safety hints without documents.

tools.name: run_graphql_operation
tools.description: Executes one approved operationId with only its declared variables. Mutation entries require confirmed=true.
notes: The operation manifest is the capability matrix and declares rootField, variable GraphQL types, exact scopes, readOnly, destructive, openWorld, and requiresConfirmation metadata.
The single static tool descriptor is conservative because safety and scopes vary by operationId; exact authorization is enforced from the selected manifest entry.
Authorization headers and cookies are never copied into the in-process GraphQL context.
